title: TokenDoor
description: A token-gated access control gate that authorises a subject to cross a named boundary (e.g. a boot phase, provisioning step, or control-plane surface) when it presents a valid CapabilityToken.

specVersion.description: Spec version of this document, e.g. "2.0.0".

doorName.description: Human-readable name of the gate, e.g. "boot-recovery-entry" or "provisioning-write".

requiredTokenKind.description: The CapabilityToken kind or scope string that a presenter must carry, e.g. "recovery-access" or "provisioning-write".

boundDeviceRef.description: Optional URN reference to the device or fleet this door is bound to. Null means any subject may present.

boundReleaseSetRef.description: Optional URN reference to the ReleaseSet that gates passage; the presenter's token must have been issued for this release.

policyRef.description: Optional URN reference to a Policy that further constrains access.

maxPresentations.description: Maximum number of times a valid token may be presented before the door is closed. Null means unlimited.

presentationCount.description: Number of times a valid token has been successfully presented so far.

expiresAt.description: Optional ISO 8601 date-time after which the door closes regardless of token validity.

status.description: Current state of the door: open (accepting presentations), closed (quota met or manually closed), revoked, or expired.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 date-time when this token door was created.

notes.description: Optional human-readable notes about this token door.
